evaluations.clinical_significance: Oxygen-carrying capacity of blood. Low values indicate anemia; high values may indicate polycythemia.
Indicates immune system function. Elevated in infections/inflammation; low in immunosuppression.
Essential for blood clotting. Low values increase bleeding risk; high values may increase clotting risk.
Blood sugar level. Elevated values indicate diabetes risk or diabetes.
Average blood glucose over 2-3 months. Primary diabetes diagnostic and monitoring marker.
Total cholesterol level. Elevated values increase cardiovascular disease risk.
Bad cholesterol. Primary target for cardiovascular risk reduction.
Good cholesterol. Higher values are protective against cardiovascular disease.
Type of fat in blood. Elevated levels increase cardiovascular disease risk.
Kidney function marker. Elevated values indicate impaired kidney function.
Liver enzyme. Elevated in liver damage or disease.
Thyroid function regulator. Elevated in hypothyroidism; low in hyperthyroidism.

summary.interpretation: 6 parameter(s) outside normal range. Follow-up with healthcare provider recommended.

diet: Increase iron-rich foods such as spinach, lentils, red meat, and fortified cereals
Include vitamin C rich foods (citrus fruits, tomatoes, berries) to improve iron absorption
Limit tea and coffee consumption as they inhibit iron absorption
Consider lean meat, poultry, and fish for better iron bioavailability
exercise: Avoid high-intensity workouts until hemoglobin improves
Prefer light walking (20-30 minutes daily) or gentle yoga
Gradually increase intensity as energy levels improve
recommendations.follow_up: Consult physician for complete iron studies (serum iron, ferritin, TIBC). Recheck hemoglobin after 6-8 weeks.

diet: Include high-fiber foods: whole grains, vegetables, legumes
Reduce simple carbohydrates and sugary beverages
Eat balanced meals with protein, fat, and carbohydrates
Practice portion control and regular meal timing
Limit fruit juice; prefer whole fruits
exercise: 150 minutes of moderate aerobic activity weekly
Add resistance training 2-3 times per week
Exercise after meals for better glucose management
Avoid prolonged sitting; take movement breaks every hour
Consistency is more important than intensity
recommendations.follow_up: HbA1c recheck in 3 months. Fasting and post-meal glucose monitoring. Early intervention prevents diabetes progression. Consult endocrinologist for comprehensive diabetes risk assessment.

evaluation_summary.interpretation: 6 parameter(s) outside normal range. Follow-up with healthcare provider recommended.

absorption_inhibitors: Dairy products (when consumed with iron-rich meals)

meal_timing: Eat at regular times to maintain stable metabolism
Eat smaller portions more frequently if needed
portion_control: Use smaller plates to control portions
Add carbohydrate portion (fist-sized or smaller for blood sugar concerns)
food_preparation: Prepare meals at home to control ingredients
Use cooking methods: boil, bake, grill, steam (avoid frying)
Season with herbs and spices instead of salt
Batch cook for consistency throughout the week
shopping_tips: Shop the perimeter of grocery store (fresh foods)
Buy fresh seasonal produce when possible

exercise_plan.intensity_level: Able to talk but not sing during activity

frequency.rest_days: At least 1-2 days per week for recovery

duration.note: Can be split into 10-15 min sessions if needed

precautions: Avoid sudden movements or position changes
Stop if dizziness or shortness of breath occurs
Avoid exercising when feeling particularly fatigued

week_5_to_8: Re-evaluate after 8 weeks with healthcare provider
month_3_plus: Incorporate new activities to prevent boredom
Regular health monitoring and provider consultation

tips: Sleep is crucial for red blood cell production
Maintain consistent sleep schedule (even on weekends)
Create dark, cool, quiet sleep environment

daily_practices: Deep breathing exercises: 5 minutes, 2-3 times daily
condition_specific: Regular stress management supports overall health
Develop coping strategies and support network
activities: Nature walks or time in parks

habits_to_adopt: Consistent meal times and portion control
habit_change_tips: Start with one habit at a time
Set specific, measurable goals
Track progress daily or weekly
Identify triggers and prepare alternatives
Build habits gradually (gradual change is sustainable)
Use positive reinforcement, not punishment
Involve family for accountability and support
Expect setbacks; get back on track immediately

home_environment: Keep healthy foods visible and accessible
Remove tempting unhealthy foods from home
Create a dedicated exercise space
Maintain comfortable temperature for sleep
Minimize noise and light disruptions
Stock kitchen with cooking supplies for healthy meals
work_environment: Stay hydrated with water throughout the day
Set reminders for medication if needed
social_support: Inform family and friends about health goals
Find accountability partner or health buddy
Join support groups (online or in-person)
Consider working with nutritionist or health coach
Communicate needs to healthcare team
Avoid peer pressure regarding unhealthy behaviors

info: These recommendations are based on detected patterns and general medical guidelines. Individual response may vary based on personal medical history, medications, and other factors.

formatted_output:
======================================================================
MEDICAL RISK ANALYSIS & LIFESTYLE RECOMMENDATIONS
======================================================================

📋 DETECTED CONDITIONS:
  1. Anemia Indicator
  2. Diabetes Risk
  3. Kidney Function Alert
  4. Liver Function Alert

----------------------------------------------------------------------
PERSONALIZED LIFESTYLE RECOMMENDATIONS:
----------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Anemia Indicator
   Severity: Moderate

   🥗 DIET:
      • Increase iron-rich foods such as spinach, lentils, red meat, and fortified cereals
      • Include vitamin C rich foods (citrus fruits, tomatoes, berries) to improve iron absorption
      • Limit tea and coffee consumption as they inhibit iron absorption
      • Consider lean meat, poultry, and fish for better iron bioavailability

   💪 EXERCISE:
      • Avoid high-intensity workouts until hemoglobin improves
      • Prefer light walking (20-30 minutes daily) or gentle yoga
      • Rest adequately between exercise sessions
      • Gradually increase intensity as energy levels improve

   📅 FOLLOW-UP: Consult physician for complete iron studies (serum iron, ferritin, TIBC). Recheck hemoglobin after 6-8 weeks.

2. Diabetes Risk
   Severity: Moderate

   🥗 DIET:
      • Choose low glycemic index foods
      • Include high-fiber foods: whole grains, vegetables, legumes
      • Reduce simple carbohydrates and sugary beverages
      • Eat balanced meals with protein, fat, and carbohydrates
      • Practice portion control and regular meal timing
      • Limit fruit juice; prefer whole fruits

   💪 EXERCISE:
      • 150 minutes of moderate aerobic activity weekly
      • Add resistance training 2-3 times per week
      • Exercise after meals for better glucose management
      • Avoid prolonged sitting; take movement breaks every hour
      • Consistency is more important than intensity

   📅 FOLLOW-UP: HbA1c recheck in 3 months. Fasting and post-meal glucose monitoring. Early intervention prevents diabetes progression. Consult endocrinologist for comprehensive diabetes risk assessment.

3. Kidney Function Alert
   Severity: Moderate

   🥗 DIET:
      • Maintain balanced, nutritious diet with whole foods

   💪 EXERCISE:
      • Maintain regular moderate physical activity

   📅 FOLLOW-UP: Consult healthcare provider for condition-specific guidance

4. Liver Function Alert
   Severity: Moderate

   🥗 DIET:
      • Maintain balanced, nutritious diet with whole foods

   💪 EXERCISE:
      • Maintain regular moderate physical activity

   📅 FOLLOW-UP: Consult healthcare provider for condition-specific guidance

5. WBC - High
   Severity: Moderate

   🥗 DIET:
      • Focus on anti-inflammatory foods (berries, fatty fish, leafy greens)
      • Avoid processed foods and excessive sugar
      • Stay well-hydrated

   💪 EXERCISE:
      • Consult healthcare provider before starting new exercise program
      • Start with light to moderate activity as tolerated
      • Gradually increase intensity under medical supervision
      • Monitor symptoms and adjust activity accordingly

   📅 FOLLOW-UP: Discuss WBC levels with healthcare provider. Monitor and retest as recommended.

6. TSH - High
   Severity: Severe

   🥗 DIET:
      • Maintain balanced, nutritious diet
      • Consult healthcare provider for parameter-specific dietary guidance
      • Consider referral to registered dietitian

   💪 EXERCISE:
      • Consult healthcare provider before starting new exercise program
      • Start with light to moderate activity as tolerated
      • Gradually increase intensity under medical supervision
      • Monitor symptoms and adjust activity accordingly

   📅 FOLLOW-UP: Discuss TSH levels with healthcare provider. Monitor and retest as recommended.

----------------------------------------------------------------------
GENERAL LIFESTYLE RECOMMENDATIONS:
----------------------------------------------------------------------

Sleep:
   • Aim for 7-9 hours of quality sleep nightly
   • Maintain consistent sleep schedule
   • Create a cool, dark, quiet sleep environment
   • Avoid screens 30 minutes before bedtime

Stress Management:
   • Practice meditation or mindfulness (10-15 minutes daily)
   • Engage in relaxation techniques (deep breathing, progressive muscle relaxation)
   • Maintain social connections and support networks
   • Seek professional help if experiencing chronic stress or anxiety

Preventive Care:
   • Annual health checkups and screening tests based on age and risk factors
   • Maintain vaccinations as recommended
   • Avoid smoking and limit alcohol consumption
   • Regular monitoring of vital signs (blood pressure, weight)

======================================================================
⚠️  MEDICAL DISCLAIMER:
======================================================================
DISCLAIMER: This recommendation engine provides general lifestyle guidance based on clinical patterns and guidelines. It does NOT replace professional medical advice. Always consult with qualified healthcare providers for personalized medical recommendations, diagnosis, and treatment. The user assumes full responsibility for any decisions made based on this information.
======================================================================
